compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
PASS-1 review — criterion T1 (Prior-art / novel-architecture justification, OVERLAY): CLEAN (no findings).

Affirmations:
- T1(a) prior art considered / not reinventing: The plan builds ISF as one more criterion inside the EXISTING pluggable plan-review criterion framework (criterion = a front-matter prompt file in the da27 prompt library + a routing entry in `criteria_routing.json`), not a new architecture. The registry docstring (`src/rebar/llm/plan_review/registry.py`) already enumerates ISF as a member criterion and documents the `exec: 1-TURN | 2-STEP | AGENT` tiers — so "single-turn/2-STEP, NOT agent" SELECTS an existing tier rather than inventing one. Substantial matching scaffolding already exists: `src/rebar/llm/reviewers/plan_review_ISF.md` (the ISF prompt), `orchestrator._linked_session_log(...)` with a `summarized` flag, and the "fires only when a session log is linked" gate (`coverage["isf"] = {"ran": False, "reason": "no linked session_log"}`). The oversized-log SUMMARY fallback reuses the existing context-window lever. This is a well-trodden in-house pattern.
- T1(b) novelty justified: no NOVEL architectural pattern is introduced — nothing to justify under Rule-of-Three / anti-repackaging.
- T1(c) unverified capability assertions: none load-bearing; the "frontier model / single-turn / summarize-when-oversized" claims map to existing runner/prompt machinery.

Not-applicable rationale: T1 is an OVERLAY that applies only when a bright line is crossed (external integration, unfamiliar dependency, security/auth, novel architecture, performance/scalability target, migration). None fires here — this is an additive use of an established internal framework. Per T1's anti-FP clause ("a well-trodden pattern needs no prior-art search; not-applicable when no bright-line fires"), this chunk PASSES with no findings.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
PASS 1 review (rubric T3 — Integration feasibility [overlay]): NOT-APPLICABLE / PASS. No findings.

The T3 overlay engages only when a plan integrates an EXTERNAL API/CLI/service/library or asserts a capability it has not used before. Every integration this ISF criterion-build plan depends on is an internal, already-used rebar seam (T3 ANTI-FP: "an internal, already-used integration is not-applicable"):

- Frontier-model single-turn LLM call: runs on the SHIPPED provider-agnostic pydantic_ai runtime (src/rebar/llm/config.py); the ISF call itself already exists — pass1_isf() in src/rebar/llm/plan_review/passes.py (execution_mode="single_turn", NOT agentic, fed plan + session_log_text). No new external API asserted.
- Eval-suite validation: the Inspect-AI eval seam is SHIPPED (src/rebar/llm/eval.py, .eval.yaml specs, CI-gated, per epic e6d9 / ticket 7284 comments); an ISF eval spec already exists (src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/plan-review-isf-finder.eval.yaml). The eval harness + grader model are owned by 7284 per the relates_to split, not introduced here.
- Linked session-log access + oversized-log SUMMARY fallback: internal rebar infrastructure (rebar session-logs / MCP recent_session_logs; orchestrator.py resolves the linked log; summarize_for_isf() is an internal LLM text call). The summary + reduced-confidence fallback is the plan's own mitigation for the context-window limit — internal runtime input handling, not a new external capability.

No external subcommand/endpoint to verify (MATCH/MISMATCH/UNVERIFIED n/a), no auth/HTTPS precondition newly introduced, and no asserted-but-unverified external capability the plan depends on — so no SPIKE-routing gap. The overlay does not engage.

Affirmations (T3): not-applicable — all integrations are internal + already-used (pydantic_ai runtime, shipped Inspect-AI eval seam, rebar session-log reads); the criterion + summary-fallback are already realized in shipped code, with no new external API/CLI/service/library asserted.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
PASS-1 review (criterion G6 — approach soundness / anti-patterns / alternative-selection). One coaching finding; the rest of G6 is affirmed.

AFFIRMATIONS (G6 criteria this plan passes):
- FITNESS-FOR-PURPOSE: ISF targets the named problem directly — silent descoping of an EXTERNALLY-expressed requirement (the gate's plan-internal blind spot) — not a proxy. The "Why" frames it precisely and the descriptor distinguishes it from E3 (plan-vs-title) vs ISF (plan-vs-original-intent).
- MECHANISM CORRECTNESS (edge/empty/failure): the trigger-gating and fallbacks are complete and sound. No linked session log -> not-applicable / skip (never fabricate an intent baseline); read errors -> best-effort skip that never crashes the review; oversized log -> summarize the LOG ONLY (never the plan) under a token budget, RECORD it, and carry reduced confidence. This matches the implemented data-flow (_linked_session_log + summarize_for_isf + pass1_isf in src/rebar/llm/plan_review/{orchestrator,passes}.py).
- APPROACH SELECTION (defensible; alternatives discarded): I generated two structurally-different alternatives — (a) an AGENTIC/tool-using ISF that fetches the log on demand, and (b) folding intent-fidelity into existing E3 rather than a dedicated criterion. The plan explicitly forecloses (a) ("NOT agent/tool-using", deterministic) and the descriptor explicitly separates ISF from E3. The chosen single-turn-fed-context approach is at-least-as-good on determinism, blast-radius, and testability. PASS.
- POSITIVE RATIONALE present: "has distinct mechanics, so it gets its own child" (own-child rationale), frontier model justified by "large session-log context", non-agentic justified as "deterministic".

FINDING (G6 — mechanism/data-flow completeness):
- [ ] Make the "ticket graph" feed explicit in the data-flow / AC. The What and the ISF descriptor both state ISF is "FED the linked session log + the pre-resolved TICKET GRAPH" and step (2) is "check the plan + its ticket graph", but AC #1 only enumerates "fed session log + ticket graph" as a label without specifying HOW the ticket graph reaches the model. In the current implementation pass1_isf is fed only the session-log text (instructions) + the plan via the {{plan}} system-prompt var (src/rebar/llm/plan_review/passes.py:304-318, _resolve_system at :171-180); no pre-resolved ticket-graph blob is passed. State in the AC where the ticket graph is rendered into the ISF input so a requirement honored by a SIBLING/CHILD ticket (not the plan body) isn't mis-flagged as silently dropped — otherwise step (2)'s "honored by the plan + ticket graph" cannot actually be evaluated. 
  evidence: What bullet "FED the linked session log + the pre-resolved ticket graph"; AC #1; criteria_routing.json:1161 check "honored by the plan + ticket graph"; ABSENCE in pass1_isf data-flow (only session_log_text + {{plan}} are passed).
  criteria: [G6]

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
PASS 1 review — T8 (LLM/prompt structural-completeness probe) [overlay]. APPLICABLE: this plan defines an LLM evaluator (the ISF criterion: prompts, a session-log-fed finder, an output/findings shape, a confidence-state mechanic). Tool-grounded against the shipped ISF machinery. One PROVEN structural gap; the rest of the probe passes.

AFFIRMATIONS (criteria this chunk satisfies — positive feedback, not findings):
- enum_vocab_defined / instruction_locality: every referenced artifact EXISTS and is fully specified. ISF has a routing entry (`src/rebar/llm/plan_review/criteria_routing.json` → `"ISF"`: exec=2-STEP, facet=intent-provenance, full checklist) so `registry._descriptor_from_prompt("ISF")` loads (it is in `CANONICAL_LLM` and would RegistryError without that entry). The finder prompt (`reviewers/plan_review_isf_finder.md`) and summarizer prompt (`reviewers/plan_review_isf_summarizer.md`) both exist. The findings output schema (`plan_review_findings`) and its no-severity/no-confidence Pass-1 shape are defined in `passes.py` / `findings.py`.
- protocol_colocated: the plan's "single-turn / 2-STEP" decision rule IS co-located with the schema that needs it — the ISF finder prompt states the two steps inline ("(1) extract the discrete expressed requirements...; (2) check the plan vs each"), and the ANTI-FP rule (descope-with-rationale is NOT a finding) is in the same prompt. The finder being declared `execution_mode: single_turn` while the criterion is `2-STEP` is NOT a gap — the 2 steps are the in-prompt extract→check protocol, correctly co-located. PASS.
- fallback_specified (oversized log): the summary fallback is fully specified end-to-end — `_linked_session_log` (orchestrator.py) gates on a token budget, calls `summarize_for_isf`, records `coverage["isf"]={ran,summarized}`, and the plan-is-never-summarized invariant holds (only the log is fed to the summarizer). The not-applicable fallback (no linked session_log → skip, never fabricate a baseline) is also specified (routing `trigger` + orchestrator `coverage["isf"]={ran:False, reason:"no linked session_log"}`).

FINDING (T8 — counter/state mechanic with no defined processing rule):

- [ ] Define HOW ISF's "reduced confidence" actually affects the verdict: the `_reduced_confidence` flag set on each summarized-log finding (`passes.py:333`) has NO downstream consumer — neither Pass-2 (`pass2_verify`) nor the deterministic Pass-3 (`pass3_decide`, validity×impact) reads it, so a summary-derived ISF finding currently decides identically to a full-log one despite the plan's "carry REDUCED CONFIDENCE (supporting context only — never the plan)". Specify the processing rule (e.g. a Pass-3 priority/validity dampener or a forced-advisory posture for summarized findings) and co-locate it with the decision path, OR state explicitly in the plan that "reduced confidence" is provenance-only (surfaced in coverage/evidence, no decision effect) — so AC2 ("documented SUMMARY + reduced confidence") names a defined, testable effect rather than an inert flag.
  - criteria: [T8]
  - location: AC line 2 ("Oversized-log fallback: documented SUMMARY + reduced confidence"); src/rebar/llm/plan_review/passes.py:333 (`_reduced_confidence`); src/rebar/llm/plan_review/passes.py:435 (`pass3_decide`)
  - evidence: "carry REDUCED CONFIDENCE (supporting context only — never the plan)" (plan What/Size-handling); `_reduced_confidence` is set at passes.py:333 but `grep _reduced_confidence` finds NO reader in passes.py (Pass-2/Pass-3) or orchestrator.py beyond the set site + a test; `pass3_decide` computes priority = validity × impact with no confidence term and never inspects the finding's private flags. This is the T8 "counter/state increment with ambiguous placement" / "decision rule not co-located with the state it governs" antipattern.
  - suggested_fix: (empty — the right resolution is a design choice between "make it a real dampener" vs "declare it provenance-only"; the author should pick and pin it in the plan + co-locate the rule.)

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
PASS-1 review — criterion T1 (Prior-art / novel-architecture justification, OVERLAY): CLEAN / NOT-APPLICABLE (no findings). Concurs with and re-grounds the prior T1 disposition.

T1 is an overlay that engages only when a bright line is crossed (external integration, unfamiliar dependency, security/auth, novel architecture, performance/scalability target, migration). None fires here — ISF is an ADDITIVE use of an established in-house framework — so per T1's anti-FP clause ("a well-trodden pattern needs no prior-art search; not-applicable when no bright-line fires") the chunk PASSES.

Affirmations (T1):
- (a) Prior art considered / not reinventing: The plan builds ISF as one more criterion inside the EXISTING pluggable plan-review framework — a criterion = a routing entry in criteria_routing.json + a front-matter prompt file under reviewers/ (the same pattern as F1/E2/G5/T5a-e, plan_review_*.md) + a registry descriptor. The registry (src/rebar/llm/plan_review/registry.py:30-41) documents the exec tiers ("1-TURN"|"2-STEP"|"AGENT"), so the plan's "single-turn/2-STEP, NOT agent" SELECTS an existing tier rather than inventing one. Substantial scaffolding already exists: pass1_isf() (src/rebar/llm/plan_review/passes.py:292-318, execution_mode single_turn / NOT agentic), its orchestrator wiring (orchestrator.py:653), a _reduced_confidence tag for the summarized-log path, and a covering unit test (tests/unit/test_plan_review.py:533). This is a well-trodden internal pattern, not a repackaging of an external artifact.
- (b) Novelty justified (anti-repackaging / Rule-of-Three): no NOVEL architectural pattern is introduced — nothing to justify. The summary + reduced-confidence fallback is a routine reuse of the context-window lever, not a new mechanism.
- (c) Unverified capability assertions resolved: none load-bearing. "frontier-model / single-turn / summarize-when-oversized" all map to existing runner/prompt machinery (RunRequest in passes.py); no "library supports X" claim depends on an unverified external capability (the eval-suite/Inspect-AI seam is owned by 7284 per the relates_to split and is already shipped).

No findings for T1.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
PASS 1 review — T8 (LLM / prompt structural-completeness probe) [overlay]: APPLICABLE / PASS with 1 coaching finding.

This plan defines an LLM criterion (ISF: single-turn/2-STEP frontier evaluator, output findings schema, summary-fallback sub-step), so the overlay engages. I tool-verified the referenced structures and they are present and fully specified:
- ISF descriptor exists and co-locates its 2-STEP decision protocol with the criterion (src/rebar/llm/reviewers/plan_review_ISF.md; finder prompt plan_review_isf_finder.md). [affirms: protocol/decision-rule co-located with the schema that needs it]
- Output schema is a named, resolvable contract (`plan_review_findings`; passes.py pass1_isf output_schema). No referenced schema/enum with an undefined value vocabulary. [affirms: no undefined-but-referenced enum]
- Session-log gating + no-log/read-error fallback are specified (orchestrator._linked_session_log returns (None, False) → ISF skipped, never crashes). The oversized-log SUMMARY sub-step + reduced-confidence tagging are specified and wired (summarize_for_isf; _reduced_confidence flag); on summarizer failure ISF is skipped gracefully. [affirms: fallback for an incomplete/failed sub-step specified]
- No ambiguous counter/state increment; no instruction-locality/pink-elephant antipattern observed.

Coaching finding (structural, plan-level):
- [ ] Define the oversized-log threshold and the downstream consumption of "reduced confidence" in the plan text. The plan's AC says "if the linked log exceeds the escalated context window, run ISF against a SUMMARY ... carry REDUCED CONFIDENCE (supporting context only — never the plan)" but never states the budget that triggers summarization, nor what "reduced confidence" concretely does to a finding downstream (e.g., advisory-only, threshold demotion). criteria=[T8]; location="What » Size handling" / AC line 2 ("Oversized-log fallback"). evidence: plan phrase "exceeds the escalated context window" (no named budget); code computes budget = largest_window × P8_HEADROOM − P8_OUTPUT_RESERVE_TOKENS (orchestrator.py:418-420) and tags _reduced_confidence (passes.py:333) but the plan does not surface either, so an executor cannot confirm the intended trigger/effect from plan text alone. suggested_fix: add an AC clause naming the summarization trigger (the escalated-model window minus output reserve) and stating that a reduced-confidence ISF finding is advisory-only / never blocks.

Note: the summarizer output is not re-checked against the budget after summarizing (a still-oversized summary is passed through; orchestrator.py:421-427) — flagging for awareness, but this is a code-level refinement, not a plan-level structural gap, so it is not raised as a T8 finding.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
PASS-1 review (criterion G6 — approach soundness / anti-patterns / alternative-selection). One coaching finding; the rest of G6 is affirmed.

AFFIRMATIONS (G6 criteria this plan passes):
- FITNESS-FOR-PURPOSE: ISF targets the named problem directly — silent descoping of an EXTERNALLY-expressed requirement (the gate's plan-internal blind spot) — not a proxy. The "Why" frames it precisely.
- MECHANISM CORRECTNESS (edge/empty/failure handled): gating + fallbacks are sound. Fires ONLY when a session log is linked (no fabricated baseline when absent); oversized log -> summarize the LOG ONLY (never the plan) + RECORD + carry reduced confidence. This matches the shipped data-flow (summarize_for_isf + pass1_isf's summarized/_reduced_confidence tag in src/rebar/llm/plan_review/passes.py:292-336).
- APPROACH SELECTION (defensible; alternatives discarded): I generated two structurally-different alternatives — (a) an AGENTIC/tool-using ISF that fetches the log on demand, and (b) folding intent-fidelity into the existing E3 (plan-vs-title) criterion rather than a dedicated child. The plan explicitly forecloses (a) ("NOT agent/tool-using") and the descriptor separates ISF from E3; the chosen single-turn-fed-context approach is at-least-as-good on determinism, blast-radius, and testability. PASS — alternatives discarded.
- POSITIVE RATIONALE present: "has distinct mechanics, so it gets its own child" (own-child rationale) + non-agentic justified for determinism.

FINDING (G6 — mechanism/data-flow completeness):
- [ ] Make the "ticket graph" feed concrete in the data-flow / AC #1, or drop it from the descriptor. The What and AC #1 both state ISF is "FED the linked session log + the pre-resolved TICKET GRAPH", and the criterion's check is "Each expressed requirement is honored by the plan + ticket graph, or descoped WITH a stated rationale" (criteria_routing.json:1161). But the actual mechanism feeds ONLY the plan ({{plan}} var) + the session-log text as instructions — no pre-resolved ticket-graph blob reaches the model (pass1_isf, src/rebar/llm/plan_review/passes.py:304-318; _resolve_system at :171-180; the prompt plan_review_isf_finder.md renders only {{plan}} + the log). As written, step (2)'s "honored by the plan + ticket graph" cannot actually be evaluated, so a requirement satisfied by a SIBLING/CHILD ticket (not the plan body) would be mis-flagged as a silent drop — a false-fire the validation AC (#3) does not cover. Specify in AC #1 HOW the ticket graph is rendered into the ISF input (or remove "ticket graph" from the descriptor if the plan body alone is the intended comparison target).
  location: ticket 681b — "What" bullet 1 + Acceptance Criteria #1
  criteria: [G6]
  evidence: What bullet "FED the linked session log + the pre-resolved ticket graph"; AC #1 "fed session log + ticket graph"; criteria_routing.json:1161 check "honored by the plan + ticket graph"; ABSENCE in pass1_isf data-flow (passes.py:304-318 passes only session_log_text + {{plan}}; no ticket-graph blob).

comments.body:

AFFIRMATIONS (G6 criteria this plan passes):
- FITNESS-FOR-PURPOSE: ISF targets the named problem directly — silent descoping of an EXTERNALLY-expressed requirement (the gate's plan-internal blind spot), not a proxy. The "Why" frames it precisely.
- MECHANISM CORRECTNESS (edge/empty/failure handled): gating + fallbacks are sound. - APPROACH SELECTION (defensible; alternatives discarded): I generated two structurally-different alternatives — (a) an AGENTIC/tool-using ISF that fetches the log on demand, and (b) folding intent-fidelity into the existing E3 (plan-vs-title) criterion rather than a dedicated child. The What bullet 1 and AC #1 both state ISF is "FED the linked session log + the pre-resolved TICKET GRAPH", and the criterion's check is "Each expressed requirement is honored by the plan + ticket graph, or descoped WITH a stated rationale" (criteria_routing.json:1161). But the actual mechanism feeds ONLY the plan ({{plan}} var) + the session-log text as instructions — no pre-resolved ticket-graph blob reaches the model (pass1_isf, src/rebar/llm/plan_review/passes.py:304-318 passes only session_log_text + plan; _resolve_system at :171-180 renders only the {{plan}} var). As written, step (2)'s "honored by the plan + ticket graph" cannot actually be evaluated, so a requirement satisfied by a SIBLING/CHILD ticket (not the plan body) would be mis-flagged as a silent drop — a false-fire that validation AC #3 ("no false-fire on honored/justified-descope plans") does not explicitly cover. Specify in AC #1 HOW the ticket graph is rendered into the ISF input (e.g. a resolved-graph blob in instructions), or remove "ticket graph" from the descriptor if the plan body alone is the intended comparison target.

  criteria: [G6]
  evidence: What bullet "FED the linked session log + the pre-resolved ticket graph"; AC #1 "fed session log + ticket graph"; criteria_routing.json:1161 check "honored by the plan + ticket graph"; ABSENCE in pass1_isf data-flow (passes.py:304-318 passes only session_log_text + {{plan}}; no ticket-graph blob; _resolve_system at :171-180 renders only {{plan}}).

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
PASS 1 review — T8 (LLM/prompt structural-completeness probe) [overlay]: PASS / no findings. The plan defines an LLM criterion (single-turn/2-STEP ISF finder fed the session log + ticket graph), so the overlay engages. Tool-grounded probe found no proven structural gap:

(a) No undefined-but-referenced enum/schema: ISF emits the shipped `plan_review_findings` schema with the `criteria:['ISF']` value (src/rebar/llm/reviewers/plan_review_isf_finder.md L8,19; passes.py:328; registry.py:84; criteria_routing.json:1137).
(b) Decision rule co-located with its schema: the 2-step protocol (extract discrete requirements → check plan vs each, anti-FP carve-out for descope-WITH-rationale) lives in the finder prompt body (plan_review_isf_finder.md L12-19), alongside the output contract.
(c) State increment unambiguous: the reduced-confidence flag is set deterministically in pass1_isf (`_reduced_confidence=summarized`, passes.py:333) and evidence is appended when summarized (L323-324).
(d) Fallback for the incomplete/failed sub-step is specified: oversized log → summarize_for_isf with its own prompt (plan_review_isf_summarizer.md), record summary used, carry reduced confidence; the PLAN is never summarized; session-log read errors → (None, False) so ISF is skipped, never crashes (orchestrator.py:395-398, 645-660). Gating ("fires only when a session log is linked") is enforced at orchestrator.py:648-660.
(e) No instruction-locality/pink-elephant antipattern; referenced finder + summarizer reviewers and the ISF descriptor all exist and are fully specified (tool-verified above).

Affirmations (T8): value-vocabulary defined; protocol co-located with schema; counter/state placement unambiguous; failed-sub-step fallback specified; referenced agents/skills/enums exist and are fully specified.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: ISF built (passes.pass1_isf fed the linked SESSION LOG, single-turn; orchestrator._linked_session_log detects session_log deps + summary fallback with reduced confidence; ISF library prompt). VALIDATION: eval_specs/plan-review-isf-finder.eval.yaml ships recall + justified-descope false-fire cases + gold_set, wired into the Inspect-AI seam. Unit-tested + offline eval-validated. verify-completion: PASS.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
## Completion Verification Report

**Verdict: FAIL**

### AC 1 — ISF implemented per its descriptor (single-turn/2-STEP, fed session log + ticket graph, NOT agent); fires only when a session log is linked.

**NOT MET** (partial — the "ticket graph" feed is absent).

The single-turn, non-agentic ISF call exists and is correctly wired (`passes.pass1_isf`, `passes.py:292–336`, `execution_mode="single_turn"`, `_agentic: False`). The "fires only when a session log is linked" gate is implemented (`orchestrator.py:648–660`; `_linked_session_log` returns `(None, False)` when no session_log dep, producing `coverage["isf"] = {"ran": False, "reason": "no linked session_log"}`). The 2-STEP protocol lives in the finder prompt (`plan_review_isf_finder.md:15–17`).

However, AC #1 states ISF must be "FED the linked session log + the pre-resolved TICKET GRAPH." A search for `ticket_graph` across `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/` returns zero matches. `pass1_isf` is fed only `plan` + `session_log_text` (`passes.py:304–318`); `_resolve_system` renders only the `{{plan}}` variable (`passes.py:171–180`). No pre-resolved ticket-graph blob is constructed or passed to the model. Multiple reviewer comments (G6, repeated) flagged this exact gap by name and it was never resolved in code or AC text.

### AC 2 — Oversized-log fallback: documented SUMMARY + reduced confidence (never summarizes the plan).

**MET.** `summarize_for_isf()` exists (`passes.py:339–350`) and summarizes only the log text; the plan is never passed to it. `_reduced_confidence=summarized` is tagged on each finding (`passes.py:333`). A summary note is appended to evidence (`passes.py:323–324`). `coverage["isf"] = {"ran": True, "summarized": summarized}` is recorded (`orchestrator.py:650`). The summarizer prompt (`plan_review_isf_summarizer.md`) explicitly states "the plan is never summarized."

### AC 3 — Validated on labeled cases: catches a silently-descoped requirement (recall); no false-fire on honored/justified-descope plans.

**MET.** `eval_specs/plan-review-isf-finder.eval.yaml` ships recall cases ISF-R1 and ISF-R2 (expect: finding), false-fire cases ISF-F1 (honored intent) and ISF-F2 (justified-descope) (expect: pass), deterministic scorers `recall_on_silent_drop` and `no_fire_on_honored_or_justified_descope`, and a gold set covering all four cases. The spec is wired into the Inspect-AI eval seam.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: REMEDIATED AC1: ISF is now fed the PRE-RESOLVED TICKET GRAPH (orchestrator._ticket_graph_blob: parent/children/dependency links, resolved from loaded state and INJECTED — ISF stays single-turn, never fetches it) alongside the session log + plan. pass1_isf renders the graph into its instructions so a child covering an expressed requirement isn't a false 'silent drop'.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
## Completion Verification Report

**Verdict: PASS**

All three acceptance criteria are met. The prior FAIL (AC1: ticket graph feed absent) was remediated and the fix is confirmed in the code.

---

### AC 1 — ISF implemented per its descriptor (single-turn/2-STEP, fed session log + ticket graph, NOT agent); fires only when a session log is linked.

**MET.**

The previously-identified gap (no ticket-graph blob passed to the model) has been remediated:

- `_ticket_graph_blob(ctx)` is defined at `orchestrator.py:394–415`. It resolves parent, children, and dependency links from the already-loaded `PlanContext` state (not by fetching anything — ISF stays single-turn and non-agentic).
- It is injected into `pass1_isf` at `orchestrator.py:682`: `ticket_graph=_ticket_graph_blob(ctx)`.
- `pass1_isf` (`passes.py:292–345`) accepts `ticket_graph: str = ""` (`passes.py:298`), renders it into the instructions block at `passes.py:306–316` (a labeled `## Ticket graph (pre-resolved …)` section), and explicitly instructs the model to check the plan AND its ticket graph before flagging a silent drop.
- Non-agentic: `execution_mode="single_turn"` (`passes.py:326`) and `_agentic: False` (`passes.py:341`).
- Session-log gate: `orchestrator.py:673–689` — ISF runs only when `log_text` is non-None; otherwise `coverage["isf"] = {"ran": False, "reason": "no linked session_log"}`.

### AC 2 — Oversized-log fallback: documented SUMMARY + reduced confidence (never summarizes the plan).

**MET.**

- `_linked_session_log` (`orchestrator.py:418–451`) gates on a token budget and calls `passes.summarize_for_isf(runner, cfg, log_text=text)` when the log is oversized — only the log text is passed, never the plan (`passes.py:348–360`).
- `_reduced_confidence=summarized` is tagged on every finding produced from a summarized log (`passes.py:342`).
- A note is appended to evidence: `"(ISF ran against a SUMMARY of an oversized session log)"` (`passes.py:333`).
- `coverage["isf"] = {"ran": True, "summarized": summarized}` is recorded (`orchestrator.py:674`).

### AC 3 — Validated on labeled cases: catches a silently-descoped requirement (recall); no false-fire on honored/justified-descope plans.

**MET.**

`src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/plan-review-isf-finder.eval.yaml` exists with:
- Recall cases ISF-R1 (silent contradiction) and ISF-R2 (silent drop), both `expect: finding`.
- False-fire cases ISF-F1 (honored intent) and ISF-F2 (justified-descope), both `expect: pass`.
- Deterministic scorers `recall_on_silent_drop` and `no_fire_on_honored_or_justified_descope`.
- A gold set covering all four cases.
- Wired into the Inspect-AI eval seam (`prompt: plan-review-isf-finder`, `model: anthropic:claude-opus-4-8`).

compiled_state.description:
## Why
Intent-source fidelity (ISF) is the approved criterion that catches silent descoping of an EXTERNALLY-expressed
requirement (the gate's plan-internal blind spot). It has distinct mechanics, so it gets its own child.

## What
- Build ISF: a single-turn / 2-STEP, FRONTIER-model criterion, FED the linked session log + the pre-resolved
  ticket graph (NOT agent/tool-using); fires ONLY when the ticket is linked to a session log.
- Size handling: if the linked log exceeds the escalated context window, run ISF against a SUMMARY of the log,
  RECORD that a summary was used, and carry REDUCED CONFIDENCE (supporting context only — never the plan).
- Validate against the eval suite: recall (catches a seeded silently-dropped requirement), false-fire
  (no finding when the plan honors the intent or a requirement was descoped WITH a stated rationale).

## Scope
- The criterion + its session-log feeding/summary-fallback; validation via the eval suite (`7284`). THIS child
  OWNS the ISF criterion build (criterion + prompt + session-log gating); `7284` owns the eval suite/harness and
  adds ISF's eval cases — no duplication (see the `relates_to` link).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] ISF implemented per its descriptor (single-turn/2-STEP, fed session log + ticket graph, NOT agent); fires only when a session log is linked.
- [ ] Oversized-log fallback: documented SUMMARY + reduced confidence (never summarizes the plan).
- [ ] Validated on labeled cases: catches a silently-descoped requirement (recall); no false-fire on honored/justified-descope plans.
compiled_state.title: Intent-source-fidelity (ISF) criterion: build + validate
